slides.title: Topic name in plain language
slides.subtitle: What the learner will understand by the end.

visual_aid.purpose: Make the learning destination visible before details.

speaker_notes.key_message: The learner should know the destination before the lesson starts.
talk_track: Start with the learner's current mental model.
Preview the destination in plain language.

slides.title: Start with the familiar version
body: Anchor the topic to something the audience already knows.

visual_aid.purpose: Give the learner a quiet bridge into the concept.

speaker_notes.key_message: A familiar anchor lowers the cost of learning.
talk_track: Use an example the audience already understands.

slides.title: Was hard to see. Is easy to inspect.

visual_aid.purpose: Make the conceptual shift visible through contrast.

validation.expected: Both panels have parallel wording and equal visual weight.
validation.fallback: Use two sequential slides if the learner needs more time.

speaker_notes.key_message: The lesson should turn an opaque idea into an inspectable one.
talk_track: Then point to what becomes visible.
Ask whether the new terms are understandable.

visual_aid.purpose: Reveal the three moves at the learner's pace.

validation.expected: The narrative list renders as a step reveal in HTML.
validation.fallback: Show the static list and pause between bullets.

motion.purpose: Prevent the learner from reading ahead.
motion.reduced_motion_fallback: The three moves remain readable as a static list.

speaker_notes.key_message: The learner needs a reusable sequence, not a memorized paragraph.
talk_track: Explain one move at a time.
Check for confusion after the second move.
Summarize the sequence before moving on.

slides.title: Teaching close
slides.quote: A good explanation gives the learner a handle they can reuse.
slides.no_claims_reason: Closing principle rather than a sourced factual claim.

speaker_notes.key_message: The learner should leave with a handle they can reuse.
